message: bd-hiyf: harden authorized_keys base64 padding validation

 {"id":"bd-gbps","title":"Unit tests: Config parsing + env/CLI precedence","description":"Add focused unit tests for demo_showcase runtime configuration.

Why:
- The demo will have many toggles; config bugs create confusing UX and flaky tests.
- We want deterministic, documented precedence rules.

Test coverage (minimum):
- Defaults: Config created with no args/env is sane.
- CLI parsing: each flag maps correctly into Config.
- Precedence rules:
  - CLI overrides env where sensible.
  - `NO_COLOR` forces no-color unless explicitly overridden (if we support `--force-color`).
  - `--theme-file` must override `--theme` preset.
- Validation: invalid values (bad seed, bad theme name) produce clean errors.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not require a TTY.
- Failures include actionable assertions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.286603748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569277094Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569146019Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gp8c","title":"Fix Wish keyboard-interactive auth handling and session manager safety","description":"Fix Wish auth keyboard-interactive flow (russh handler missing), ensure session cleanup task is single-shot, and prevent channel count underflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:37.934158444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125584226Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125513313Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gyzr","title":"Code Quality & Reliability Hardening: Deep Review Findings","description":"# Epic: Code Quality & Reliability Hardening

## Origin
This epic captures all deficiencies identified during a comprehensive deep-dive code review
of the entire charmed_rust codebase conducted on 2026-01-27. The review examined all core
crates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, charmed_log, harmonica) for:

- Bugs and correctness issues
- Thread safety and concurrency problems
- Performance inefficiencies
- API design issues
- Code duplication and maintainability

## Scope
The findings span multiple crates with varying severity:
- **CRITICAL**: Issues that can cause panics, data loss, or security problems
- **HIGH**: Issues affecting correctness or significant performance
- **MEDIUM**: Code quality, maintainability, and minor performance issues

## Goals
1. Eliminate all sources of unexpected panics (lock poisoning, bounds checks)
2. Ensure reliable I/O with proper error handling
3. Consolidate duplicated implementations for maintainability
4. Optimize hot paths identified during review
5. Fix all identified bugs and edge cases

## Dependencies
This epic's tasks should be completed before major feature work to ensure a stable foundation.

## Quality Bar
Each fix must:
- Include regression tests where applicable
- Pass all existing tests
- Pass clippy with -D warnings
- Be reviewed for thread safety implications","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.042212490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623313430Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623247147Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","hardening","review-findings"]}
+{"id":"bd-hiyf","title":"wish authorized_keys: reject malformed base64 padding/trailing data","description":"base64_decode in authorized_keys accepts some malformed inputs: '=' can be decoded in early positions, third-char '=' does not require fourth '=', and trailing data after padding can be ignored. Harden decoder to reject invalid padding/trailing bytes and add regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:43:40.270835740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:44:53.245278768Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:44:53.245211343Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hqxx","title":"lipgloss: join center alignment off-by-one vs Go","description":"Two conformance tests fail: join_horizontal_center and join_vertical_center. Root cause: Rust calculates left_pad/top_offset directly with floor(), but Go calculates opposite side first. Fix: bottom_offset = floor(extra * (1-factor)), then top_offset = extra - bottom_offset.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AmberDog","created_at":"2026-01-30T03:47:06.431111980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:16.157165877Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:16.157097640Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-3vqi, fixed by AmberDog using round() instead of floor()","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","lipgloss"]}
 {"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516077791Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516008842Z","close_reason":"Implemented split-view docs navigation with left pane document list and right pane content viewer. Features include: Tab/Arrow keys to switch focus between panes, j/k navigation in list, vim-style scrolling in content, per-document scroll position preservation, and theme-aware rendering with highlighted borders.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikfq","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea simulator","description":"Comprehensive tests for the bubbletea test simulator.

## Current State
Simulator has tests but needs expansion for:
- Batch/sequence command execution
- Timer and tick simulation
- Window resize handling
- Error propagation

## Test Requirements

### Simulator Core
- [ ] sim_init() with various Model implementations
- [ ] sim_update() message routing
- [ ] sim_view() output capture
- [ ] Multiple update cycles

### Event Simulation
- [ ] sim_key() for all key types
- [ ] sim_mouse() for all mouse events
- [ ] sim_paste() for clipboard
- [ ] sim_resize() dimension handling

### Command Execution
- [ ] Batch commands execute all
- [ ] Sequence commands maintain order
- [ ] Nested batch/sequence combinations
- [ ] Command result message routing

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty batch handling
- [ ] Recursive updates (message spawns message)
- [ ] Large message queues
- [ ] Concurrent timer expirations

## Success Criteria
- Simulator can test any Model without real terminal
- All command types work correctly
- Timing-sensitive tests are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:35.290507703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44.380704888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:42.845133735Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","simulator","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED by VioletDog (2026-01-28)

## Summary
Added comprehensive tests and helper methods to the bubbletea simulator.

## New Helper Methods (5)
- `sim_key(c: char)` - Simulate single character key press
- `sim_key_type(key_type: KeyType)` - Simulate special keys (Enter, Esc, arrows)
- `sim_mouse(x, y, button, action)` - Simulate mouse events
- `sim_resize(width, height)` - Simulate window resize
- `sim_paste(text)` - Simulate bracketed paste

## New Tests (12)
### Event Simulation
- test_sim_key_sends_char
- test_sim_key_type_sends_special_keys
- test_sim_mouse_sends_clicks
- test_sim_resize_sends_dimensions
- test_sim_paste_sends_text

### Sequence Commands
- test_simulator_sequence_command

### Edge Cases
- test_empty_batch_does_not_panic
- test_recursive_updates_bounded
- test_large_message_queue
- test_model_mut_allows_direct_modification
- test_step_without_messages_returns_none
- test_views_accumulate

## Test Results
**22/22 simulator tests pass**","created_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44Z"}]}
